---
pagetitle: "STEPS FETP Scale-up Decision Aid | Mesfin Genie"
description: "A decision-support tool for planning the scale-up of Field Epidemiology Training Programmes in India, combining a mixed-logit preference model with cost templates and epidemiological multipliers."
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell">
<p class="eyebrow">Tool · Field epidemiology</p>
<h1>STEPS FETP Scale-up Decision Aid</h1>
<p>Scalable Training Estimation and Planning System for Field Epidemiology Training Programmes in India.</p>
</div></header>

<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow">
<h2>The problem</h2>
<p>Planning the scale-up of Field Epidemiology Training Programmes requires balancing programme design, cost, and expected health benefit under a budget constraint. STEPS lets planners test scale-up options and see the implications for endorsement, perceived value, cost, and indicative benefits.</p>
<h2>Intended users</h2>
<p>Policymakers and partners planning FETP scale-up in India, developed through work at the University of Newcastle in partnership with the World Bank.</p>
<h2>Inputs</h2>
<ul class="cv-list"><li>Programme design: tier, career incentive, mentorship intensity, delivery mode, expected response time, cost per trainee per month, cohort size, and number of cohorts.</li><li>Settings: value assumptions, planning horizon, completion rate, and exchange rate for display.</li></ul>
<h2>Outputs</h2>
<ul class="cv-list"><li>Predicted endorsement of a configuration versus opting out.</li><li>Perceived programme value (a willingness-to-pay style measure) per trainee and per cohort.</li><li>Programme, mentor-support, and opportunity costs per cohort, and a national simulation.</li><li>Saved scenarios with export for reporting.</li></ul>
<h2>Methodological basis</h2>
<p>Endorsement and perceived programme value are generated by a mixed-logit preference model estimated from a discrete choice experiment with stakeholders. Costs combine harmonised templates, and health benefits use simple epidemiological multipliers. The tool is transparent about which figures are estimates and which are assumptions.</p>
<h2>Developer</h2>
<p>Developed by Dr Mesfin Genie, Newcastle Business School, the University of Newcastle.</p>
<h2>Limitations</h2>
<p>Outputs are indicative and depend on the assumptions supplied. The averted-outbreak cost savings are drawn from the literature rather than from field estimates, and the tool is intended to support discussion rather than to replace detailed appraisal.</p>
<p><a class="button-primary" href="/STEPS-FETP-DecisionAid-V20/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Open the tool</a></p>
</div></section>
<script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"SoftwareApplication","name":"STEPS FETP Scale-up Decision Aid","applicationCategory":"Decision support tool","operatingSystem":"Web","url":"https://mesfingenie.com/STEPS-FETP-DecisionAid-V20/","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Mesfin Genie"}}</script>

```
